safety_tips.tip: Dubai is generally very safe, but stay aware of your surroundings, especially in crowded areas
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables (passport, money, gadgets) in a secure bag or hotel safe
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid walking alone in poorly lit or isolated areas at night
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use licensed taxis or ride-hailing apps (Careem, Uber)
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always wear a seatbelt while in vehicles
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Follow local traffic rules and pedestrian crossings; jaywalking is fined
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Drink bottled water to avoid dehydration; tap water is generally safe but may taste different
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use sunscreen, sunglasses, and hats to protect against sun exposure
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of overcharging in taxis – Some drivers may take longer routes; always use the meter or app-based rides
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of overpriced goods or counterfeit items in markets and malls
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of strangers offering unsolicited help, gifts, or tours may demand payment later
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep bags zipped and valuables secure in crowded areas
safety_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Etisalat is one of the largest telecom operators in UAE, offers prepaid SIMs suitable for tourists with data, local calls, and international call bundles
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: du (Emirates Integrated Telecommunications Company) provides prepaid tourist SIMs with data and voice packages
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Virgin Mobile UAE operates on the du network with flexible prepaid and postpaid plans, good for short-term visitors
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: STC UAE (Saudi Telecom Company) offers prepaid and postpaid services, often used for data-heavy packages
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: SIM cards can be bought at Dubai International Airport (Terminals 1, 2, 3) for immediate use
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Valid passport is mandatory for SIM registration, some stores may ask for visa copy
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Tourist SIMs cost around AED 100–200 for 1–2 weeks with data and local calls
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Etisalat Visitor Line offers good coverage, fast 4G/5G
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: du Tourist SIM provides affordable data packages, simple recharge options
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Virgin Mobile UAE has flexible plans, easy top-ups via app
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Kush_All Inclsuive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Etisalat offers prepaid SIMs suitable for tourists with data, local calls, and international call bundles

connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Hrishita_All Inclusive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: SIM cards can be bought at Dubai International Airport (Terminals 1, 2, 3) for immediate use
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Hrishita_All Inclusive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Valid passport is mandatory for SIM card registration
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Hrishita_All Inclusive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Tourist SIMs cost around AED 100–200 for 1–2 weeks with data and local calls
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Hrishita_All Inclusive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Popular options include Etisalat Visitor Line, du Tourist SIM, and Virgin Mobile UAE
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Hrishita_All Inclusive Guide_Dubai.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Etisalat and Du are both widely used networks. Prepaid SIM cards are available for AED 50-100 depending on the plan and can be picked up at the airport.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Dubai and Abu Dhabi- All Inclusive Guide_Rituraj.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: DU's packages are slightly cheaper than Etisalat's. Most people in Dubai use Du sim cards.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Dubai and Abu Dhabi - UAE/Dubai and Abu Dhabi- All Inclusive Guide_Rituraj.pdf
